title: Grafana Data Source Query Error Rate by Type
dataSources: `index=grafana` or `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:datasource` health and proxy query outcomes
app: Custom Grafana API poller, Grafana Reporting, or log pipeline tagging datasource_type
spl:
(index=grafana OR index=observability) sourcetype="grafana:datasource"
| eval err=if(status!="success" OR isnotnull(error_message) OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)query error|execution error|bad gateway|timeout"),1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as attempts, sum(err) as errors by _time, datasource_type, datasource_name
| eval err_rate=round(100*errors/nullif(attempts,0),2)
| where err_rate > 2 AND attempts >= 20
| sort -err_rate
description: Prometheus, InfluxDB, CloudWatch, and SQL backends fail for different reasons; error rate split by datasource_type prioritizes the right engineering owner and reveals regional or credential outages affecting one backend only.
value: Shortens MTTR for blank panels and prevents silent data gaps during provider incidents.
implementation: Emit one event per health check or sampled query from recording rules. Map `datasource_type` from Grafana’s `type` field. Join to a dashboard inventory lookup for customer impact narrative.
visualization: Stacked area (errors by type), bar chart (err_rate by datasource_name), single-value (open error budget).
grandmaExplanation: We watch our primary metrics and dashboard platform for query errors, slow views, and changes that could break the screens teams use every day.
knownFalsePositives: Error spikes during deployments, downstream issues, or after schema or contract changes. We line up releases and dependencies with the time window first.
